sources.article_title: Where to Eat & Drink & Shop for Food in Rome in 2024
sources.writer_profile: Food expert Katie Parla shares insider dining recommendations and culinary insights through her website katieparla.com. Her coverage focuses on authentic local food experiences, including comprehensive guides to eating, drinking, and food shopping in major culinary destinations like Rome.

Food writer at The Infatuation, a popular restaurant discovery platform known for its honest, accessible reviews and dining recommendations. Annie Replogle covers international dining destinations, offering insider perspectives on the best places to eat in major cities around the world.

sources.article_title: Multiple Rome restaurant reviews (Corriere della Sera contributor)
sources.writer_profile: Food critic and journalist who contributes to Corriere della Sera, Italy's leading national newspaper. Her work on camillabaresani.com features comprehensive restaurant reviews across Rome's dining scene, offering insights into the capital's culinary landscape.

sources.article_title: Where to Eat in Monti; 9 Delicious Restaurants Near Piazza Navona
sources.writer_profile: Food writer for mamalovesrome.com, specializing in Rome's dining scene with detailed neighborhood restaurant guides. Marta Correale creates comprehensive local food recommendations, covering everything from historic districts like Monti to iconic areas near major landmarks. Her work focuses on helping readers discover authentic Roman dining experiences across the city's diverse culinary landscape.

restaurants.name: Armando al Pantheon
restaurants.quote: fantastic amatriciana made with rigatoni and a sublime spaghetti alla carbonara — you can get a half-portion of each

restaurants.name: Piatto Romano
restaurants.quote: Piatto Romano, al Testaccio, è la tipica trattoria dove si vorrebbe mangiare tutti i giorni — cucina di casa ricercata, trionfi di verdure ed erbe di campo, accostamenti inconsueti che accendono la curiosità
restaurants.quote_en: located in Testaccio, the neighborhood where cucina Romana was practically invented — hence the abundance of offal on the menu, including crispy artichokes and fried meatballs from chianina beef
dishes: misticanza selvatica e portulaca in salsa di alici e sommacco
polpetti lessi ai fichi e pomodoro verde

restaurants.name: Tavernaccia Da Bruno
restaurants.quote: comfort food so good, you simply won't want to leave — one of the best places to try some of Italy's greatest hits including coda alla vaccinara and maialino

restaurants.name: Giano
restaurants.quote: you'll get a complimentary bread basket filled with crispy housemade focaccia alongside whipped ricotta with olive oil — Sicily-inspired Giano from acclaimed chef Ciccio Sultano

restaurants.name: Trattoria Trecca
restaurants.quote: their regaje di pollo e patate is the perfect example — lusciously tender chicken innards sautéed with rosemary, a dish that defines the cucina romana

restaurants.name: L'Elementare
restaurants.quote: Pizzeria L'Elementare sets the benchmark for Roman-style pizza — it strikes that perfect balance of soft, airy dough with crispy crust
dishes: supplì with fior di latte and 'nduja

restaurants.name: Romanè
restaurants.quote: their tables are lined with lace paper placemats and hand-painted ceramic plates hang on the walls, serving carbonara with crispy guanciale and pollo alla cacciatora

restaurants.name: Grappolo d'Oro
restaurants.quote: prides itself in proposing dishes that are locally sourced from small, local producers, and prepared the traditional way

restaurants.name: Capo Boi
restaurants.quote: arguably the best seafood in all of Rome, with starters like paper-thin pane carasau, a crackly Sardinian flatbread, topped with thin shavings of bottarga

restaurants.name: Dal Toscano
restaurants.quote: silky fettuccine bathed in an earthy white ragù and parmesan, as well as their thick and chewy pici noodles coated in red sauce

restaurants.name: Antico Forno Roscioli
restaurants.quote: This must-visit bakery turns out fresh-from-the-oven pizza, crispy supplì, and an irresistible selection of baked goods including ciambelline and ricotta chocolate chip crostata

restaurants.name: Da Dante
restaurants.quote: seafood spaghetti at Da Dante is so extraordinary, it might just ruin all other pasta — silky noodles glistening with garlic and the sea

restaurants.name: Trattoria Da Enzo Al 29
restaurants.quote: a simple, lively restaurant with checkered tablecloths and specials scrawled on a chalkboard, serving rigatoni alla carbonara and tiramisu with Nutella

restaurants.name: Trattoria Ai Due Ponti
restaurants.quote: Your meal starts with a spread of salami that arrives on a large wooden tray, piled high with five pounds of assorted meats — then the gricia with asparagus and suckling maialino

restaurants.name: Ristorante Rocco
restaurants.quote: A standout is their cacio e pepe, perfectly creamy and peppery with textbook al dente spaghetti

restaurants.name: Trattoria Penestri
restaurants.quote: Rustic wooden tables with vases of fresh flowers, the menu runs through all four Roman pasta pillars — carbonara, amatriciana, cacio e pepe, and pici all'aglione — plus a wonderful seared duck breast

restaurants.name: Pecorino
restaurants.quote: Even though there are white tablecloths on every table, the atmosphere is anything but pretentious at Pecorino — the creamy carbonara and maltagliati con carciofi are outstanding

restaurants.name: La Matriciana Dal 1870
restaurants.quote: La Matriciana is old-school Rome at its finest, encapsulating both the historic elegance of 1870 and the slick, suit-and-tie vibe — the amatriciana and tortellini in brodo are essential

restaurants.name: Siciliainbocca
restaurants.quote: Siciliainbocca in Prati feels like one of Palermo's bustling markets, serving eggplant caponata, softly-smoked ricotta with orange chili marmalade, and granita with brioche
dishes: smoked ricotta with orange chili marmalade

restaurants.name: Dar Filettaro
restaurants.quote: our pick for Rome's iconic baccalà — the massive filet of fried salted cod served with puntarelle tossed in anchovy vinaigrette

restaurants.name: Trattoria della Barchetta
restaurants.address: Via Giovanni Pierluigi da Palestrina 35, 00193 Roma
restaurants.quote: una cucina fastosa e per mangioni — piatti un po' vecchia maniera, molto ricchi e anche abbondanti: pizzelle con scarola, tonnarelli alla carbonara tartufata, coniglio all'ischitana con cialda di riso nero
dishes: coniglio all'ischitana con cialda di riso nero

restaurants.name: Bistrot La Differenza
restaurants.quote: una gastronomia trasformata in bistrot con un eccellente San Daniele di prosciutteria artigianale e pane del Micropanificio agricolo Triticum — gestita dai Stramaccioni, gentili e appassionati
dishes: panino con speck e gorgonzola naturale
finocchi al burro con olive e arancia

restaurants.name: Crazy Pizza
restaurants.quote: una pizza in teglia molto sottile, pomodoro e mozzarella piuttosto anonimi — per un risultato migliore conviene chiedere la Napoli con le acciughe che sanno di alici vere

restaurants.name: Antico Caffè Greco
restaurants.quote: il locale più antico di Roma, 264 anni di storia — ci sono i proprietari della licenza tutelati da 4 vincoli del Ministero dei Beni Culturali sui circa 300 quadri, statue, cimeli

restaurants.name: Santo Palato
restaurants.quote: chef Sarah Ciccolini serves at Santo Palato — her carbonara is pretty spectacular, as well as her take on offal

restaurants.name: Mazzo
restaurants.quote: After a 5-year hiatus, Mazzo has returned to the fold and the vibes and food are 10/10

restaurants.name: Menabò
restaurants.quote: Menabò, a neo-bistro in Centocelle is really excellent, plus a trip will get you out of the center

restaurants.name: Agustarello
restaurants.quote: Agustarello down the street from Piatto Romano is another solid spot with an offal-forward menu for anyone who loves the fifth quarter

restaurants.name: Osteria Bonelli
restaurants.quote: For affordable, abundant comfort food, I visit the raucous Osteria Bonelli in Torpignattara

restaurants.name: Al Moro
restaurants.quote: I also love Al Moro near the Trevi Fountain for that super old school vibe and consistently reliable cucina romana

restaurants.name: Roscioli
restaurants.quote: you'd be hard-pressed to find a better carbonara than that at Roscioli, one of Rome's great all-around destinations

restaurants.name: Baccano
restaurants.quote: chef Nabil Hadj Hassen was at Roscioli for 18 years and now runs the kitchen at Baccano, bringing with him the carbonara and cacio e pepe

restaurants.name: Tempio di Iside
restaurants.quote: the move is to eat the finest fish in town at Tempio di Iside — raw fish, excellent fried fish, and wonderful seafood pasta
restaurants.geo_matched_name: Tempio di Iside a via Labicana

restaurants.name: Trattoria Monti
restaurants.quote: Trattoria Monti is wonderful for specialties from Le Marche, run by the Camerucci family for decades

restaurants.name: Tram Tram
restaurants.quote: Tram Tram is great for fish and vegetable classics from Puglia in a setting that feels genuinely neighborhood

restaurants.name: Colline Emiliane
restaurants.quote: Colline Emiliane does amazing food from around Bologna like tortellini in brodo and tagliatelle al ragù

restaurants.name: La Torricella
restaurants.quote: There are influences from Abruzzo at La Torricella in Testaccio, particularly in the lamb dishes and the wine list

restaurants.name: Himalaya Palace
restaurants.quote: the soulful dishes of Himalaya Palace near Villa Pamphilj got me through lockdown, consistently excellent Himalayan and Nepalese cooking

restaurants.name: Enqutatash
restaurants.quote: There is incredible Ethiopian and Eritrean food at Enqutatash on the Via Prenestina

restaurants.name: Sinosteria
restaurants.quote: Sinosteria in Viale Marconi does ace Chinese regional cuisines and has a very good wine list

restaurants.name: Carnal
restaurants.quote: Roy Caceres' Carnal does good tacos and arepas in Prati, a welcome addition to Rome's Latin American scene

restaurants.name: El Jalapeño
restaurants.quote: El Jalapeño does very tasty tacos in the Mercato Irnerio up on the Via Aurelia

restaurants.name: Tianci Chongqing Farm Hotpot
restaurants.quote: Tianci Chongqing Farm Hotpot does really excellent Chongqing-style hot pot, one of the best Chinese restaurants in Rome

restaurants.name: JiaMo Lab
restaurants.quote: JiaMo Lab in Piazza Fiume makes delectable northwestern Chinese sandwiches, a rare regional Chinese offering in Rome

restaurants.name: I Quintili
restaurants.quote: Neapolitan import I Quintili is fun and does delicious fritti alongside their excellent Neapolitan pizza

restaurants.name: Seu Pizza Illuminati
restaurants.quote: Seu Pizza Illuminati at the edge of Trastevere serves avant-garde pizzas in an open space, one of Rome's most creative pizzerias

restaurants.name: Mordi e Vai
restaurants.quote: Go to Mordi e Vai in the Mercato di Testaccio for brisket and chicory sandwiches, artichoke and pecorino, and kidney and onion

restaurants.name: Supplì
restaurants.quote: Supplì, the institution in Trastevere, serves very good supplì — the definitive Roman fried rice ball

restaurants.name: Legs
restaurants.quote: Legs in Centocelle serves the city's best fried chicken sandwiches

restaurants.name: I-Gio
restaurants.quote: Korean restaurant I-Gio in Trastevere is delightful and opens at 6:30PM, one of the city's best Asian restaurants

restaurants.name: Cuoco e Camicia
restaurants.quote: plates assembled and presented with care, with options ranging from more traditional to experimental — a beautiful spot in the cobbled streets close to the Colosseum

restaurants.name: Pizza della Madonna dei Monti
restaurants.quote: great take out pizza and supplì place perfect for a quick lunch — pizza is sold by weight all day long

restaurants.name: La Taverna dei Monti
restaurants.quote: excellent and popular traditional trattoria in Monti, ideal to taste Rome staples — carbonara, cacio e pepe, lamb and more

restaurants.name: Temakinho
restaurants.quote: fun, colorful, tasty Brazilian-Japanese fusion restaurant, perfect for when you need a break from Italian cuisine

restaurants.name: Maharaja
restaurants.quote: popular Indian restaurant in Monti that has been operating for decades — one of the best addresses for Indian food in the center of Rome

restaurants.name: Polpetta
restaurants.quote: fun, good quality, informal restaurant with an unusual mission: to showcase you can make all types of food into a tasty ball

restaurants.name: Fuorinorma
restaurants.quote: great little place for lunch or aperitivo, with sandwiches — try the porchetta one — platters, beer and wine

restaurants.name: Camillo a Piazza Navona dal 1890
restaurants.quote: the restaurant has lovely dishes that mix traditional roman staples such as carbonara with some Asian inspired dishes

restaurants.name: Mimi e Coco
restaurants.quote: one of the best restaurants in this area if you want to taste real carbonara — I have tried many and theirs truly is special

restaurants.name: Taverna Agape
restaurants.quote: serves traditional roman staples — cacio e pepe, gricia, oxtail and more — but also a good selection of seafood based dishes

restaurants.name: Felice a Testaccio
restaurants.quote: the dish is made right in front of you, so you can see how the smooth cacio cream comes to be — a Testaccio institution for Roman classics

restaurants.name: Spirito Divino
restaurants.quote: the restaurant still has an ancient Trastevere cellar open to visitors where you can see thousands of bottles with highly selected wines

restaurants.name: Antica Pesa
restaurants.quote: The restaurant still retains the ancient charm of this special location but mixes it with elegant ambiance — one of Trastevere's most storied dining rooms

restaurants.name: Romeow Cat Bistro
restaurants.quote: Playful, creative, and fully plant-based, this bistro is lovely, welcoming, and a truly unique Roman experience
